text.title: event-sourcing-agentic Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for event-sourcing-agentic as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: CRUD-shaped events: agent emits UserUpdated, OrderUpdated, OrderUpdated_v2 — flat state snapshots.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force naming "what business action happened" and reject Updated/Changed patterns in lint. Snapshot events destroy the audit trail — they carry no business intent.

failure-mode.description: Mutating state outside apply: agent calls self.status = "placed" in command handlers because it feels natural.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The aggregate must mutate state ONLY in apply handlers. Command handlers emit events; replay invokes apply. State mutation in command methods breaks replay determinism.

failure-mode.description: Forgotten expected_version: code-gen agent saves events without optimistic-concurrency check. Replays clobber concurrent writes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include expected_version parameter in repository save signatures. The lint check for missing expected_version in event-catalog-lint.py catches this pattern.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated event-store APIs: agents invent event_store.append_one(...), repo.put_events(...), bus.fire(...) that do not exist in the target library.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the exact API in the prompt and provide the imports. Include a concrete example of the target library's append() call signature.

failure-mode.description: Snapshot misuse: agent persists current state as a snapshot AND treats it as truth, skipping event replay "if snapshot exists". This breaks event-versioning.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always rebuild snapshot when event schema bumps. Snapshots are a performance cache, never the source of truth. Replay from events after the snapshot version must always be performed.

failure-mode.description: Projection writes business logic: agent writes if order.total > 1000: send_email(...) in projection handler.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Projections are passive UPSERTs. Emit a domain event for "high-value order" and drive the action from a separate handler. Business logic in projections fires on every rebuild.

failure-mode.description: PII leaks into events: agent copies command payload directly into events including shipping_address, email, card_token.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a redact step and a per-event PII-classification field. Lint blocks PII without a shred plan. See event-sourcing-versioning for crypto-shredding and PII externalization strategies.

failure-mode.description: Cross-aggregate handlers: agent writes place_order(...) that loads Customer, Inventory, Payment, and mutates all four.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: That is a saga, not a command handler. Refactor into a process manager. Cross-aggregate mutations are not atomic and produce ghost states.

failure-mode.description: Tests on aggregate getters: agent tests aggregate.status == "placed" after replay instead of testing event emission.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Better: test that apply(events) produces the same state as the original aggregate (round-trip) and that command produces the expected events list. This tests the actual event-sourcing contract.
